researcher_note: Sources gathered via web search on 2026-05-09/10 for the novel-writing+world-simulation app frontend audit. Each card maps to inline citations like [s12] in research_novel_writing_tools.md.

sources.title: What is Story Bible — Sudowrite Documentation
sources.key_claim: Story Bible holds genre, style, synopsis, characters, worldbuilding, and outline as named fields that the AI silently references during generation.
sources.relevance: Reference for verb-based AI grammar and structured-context pattern — basis for our chapter-generation IA recommendation.

sources.title: Sudowrite Review 2026: Tested w/ 3 Stories — Best AI for Fiction? (Nerdynav)
sources.key_claim: Sudowrite organizes its UX around three named verbs (Write/Describe/Brainstorm) and a Canvas surface for plot/character relationships; users praise the verb-based grammar and find Canvas useful but occasionally disorganized.
sources.relevance: Validates verb-based AI surfacing and shows the limits of unconstrained mind-map/canvas surfaces.

sources.title: Novelcrafter Changelog & Codex UX (feedback.novelcrafter.com)
sources.key_claim: Novelcrafter's UX is built around 'context-at-a-glance', a 3-panel layout (manuscript / story-beats or Codex / inspector), with auto-detection of Codex references in prose and per-scene context selection for AI prompts.
sources.relevance: Direct reference for our scene-editing workspace 3-pane shell and auto-mention detection.

sources.title: Novelcrafter Review: Powerful for Fiction Writers, Frustrating to Set Up (April 2026)
sources.key_claim: Most-cited complaints: BYOK setup feels like 'developer work, not writer work', Codex maintenance is 'exhausting', and detailed outlines often don't reach the model — the platform claims more than the prompt builder uses.
sources.relevance: Anti-pattern catalog: don't gate first-run on API keys; show users exactly what context the AI received.

sources.title: Sudowrite Trustpilot Reviews (UK)
sources.key_claim: Reports of glitches (Beats feature failing while consuming credits), slow support, and Terms-of-Service ambiguity around IP ownership of user inputs.
sources.relevance: Reliability and trust gripes feed our 'show what the AI did and what it cost' principle.

sources.title: NovelAI Lorebook Documentation
sources.key_claim: Lorebook entries are inserted into the AI's context only when their trigger keywords appear in recent text — a hybrid of always-on memory and manual selection.
sources.relevance: Pattern reference for our story-memory workspace: keyword-triggered auto-injection on top of manual selection.

sources.title: NovelAI Review (2026): Great for World-Building, But There's a Catch (Shakespeare AI Blog)
sources.key_claim: Even at Opus-tier 8,192-token context, only ~6–7k recent words are visible; the interface gives no warning when context rolls over, causing sudden 'character amnesia'. Users manually correct narrative flow constantly.
sources.relevance: Anti-pattern: silent context rollover. Drives our 'context warning' recommendation.

sources.title: Integrating Scrivener's Binder, Corkboard, and Outliner — Literature & Latte
sources.key_claim: Binder, Corkboard, and Outliner are three views of the same underlying tree; rearranging in one view is reflected in all three. The Inspector holds metadata (synopsis, keywords, notes) — the corkboard card synopsis IS the Inspector synopsis.
sources.relevance: Canonical reference for the 'same-data, multiple-projections' IA principle — our top recommendation.

sources.title: The Inspector (Scrivener 3's revised user interface) — ScrivenerVirgin
sources.key_claim: The Inspector is the right-hand pane that holds metadata about the currently open document; it survives across editor changes.
sources.relevance: Validates inspector-as-metadata pattern (not duplicated prose).

sources.title: Ulysses Review — Codeless / Selfpublishing.com / Vice
sources.key_claim: Distraction-free first-class modes (typewriter, dark, focus, full-screen). Filters create live folders; library is a first-class IA. Cons: Apple-only, subscription expensive, almost no formatting capability.
sources.relevance: Reference for first-class focus modes and saved-view IA pattern.

sources.title: Plottr Review (Reedsy & Kindlepreneur)
sources.key_claim: Plottr's strength is template-scaffolded story structures (Hero's Journey, Three-Act, Save the Cat) and drag-and-drop timeline cards. Weakness: no writing surface, performance degrades past a card threshold, limited export.
sources.relevance: Reference for template-driven empty states and 'plan view must support inline edit or jump-to-edit'.

sources.title: Obsidian for Fiction Writers — Loreteller / XPress Book Box / Iain F Macleod
sources.key_claim: Obsidian out of the box is not a writing tool; it's a note engine with no opinion. New users link aggressively because the graph 'looks impressive', but meaningful links should connect entities with narrative relationships, otherwise the graph is noise.
sources.relevance: Anti-pattern reference: a freeform graph without curation is not navigable. Drives our typed/filtered atlas recommendation.

sources.title: Why I stopped using Notion — UX Planet & Hacker News thread
sources.key_claim: Notion's flexibility is its weakness: users are overwhelmed by complexity. Adding a single block reveals a long scroll of options. Steep learning curve, decision fatigue.
sources.relevance: Anti-pattern: infinite generality has a UX cost. Validates our 6-fixed-workspaces approach.

sources.title: Sudowrite Pricing & Credit Complaints (CheckThat.ai, costbench.com, feedback.sudowrite.com)
sources.key_claim: Heavy users report 4 chapters / 7–18k words consuming 2M credits; the credit system is 'opaque', costs vary by model and length; users repeatedly request unlimited or roll-over plans.
sources.relevance: Anti-pattern: hidden/variable AI cost. Drives our 'predictable cost telemetry' principle.

sources.title: ShortlyAI / Jasper acquisition & Jasper Trustpilot complaints
sources.key_claim: ShortlyAI was absorbed into Jasper's Boss Mode; Jasper itself has billing complaints (continued billing after cancellation, no use of already-paid days after pause).
sources.relevance: Pricing/trust UX lesson: cancellation and pause UX matters.

sources.title: Plot Bunni — FOSS Novel Writing Suite (plotbunni.com / GitHub)
sources.key_claim: Open-source, browser-based, IndexedDB-local storage, BYOK to OpenRouter/LMStudio/Koboldcpp; 100% free.
sources.relevance: Validates local-first storage and open-source as trust features.

sources.title: Notion Performance & Information Architecture critiques (hackceleration.com, capterra)
sources.key_claim: Performance degrades past 5,000 records (load times 3–5s/page). Users build for imagined future workflows rather than current behavior; IA should reflect cognitive patterns, not data normalization.
sources.relevance: Performance-budget warning for our atlas/knowledge view; IA principle for cognitive-pattern-first design.

sources.title: Logseq vs Roam Research — Travis Chan / Sweet Setup / Saasweep
sources.key_claim: Outliner paradigm has a steep learning curve; many users never reach proficiency. Logseq performance issues past thousands of pages. Roam $15/mo for what Logseq does free.
sources.relevance: Anti-pattern: outliner-everywhere is overkill for fiction; bidirectional links should be sparse/explicit.

sources.title: Where should AI sit in your UI? — UX Collective / Sharang Sharma
sources.key_claim: Maps emerging AI UI patterns: persistent side panel for collaborative work; inline ghost text for short completions; inline action menu at selection for span operations.
sources.relevance: Direct guidance for AI-surfacing decisions across our chapter, scene, branch, and memory workspaces.

sources.title: AI UX Patterns | Inline Action — Shape of AI
sources.key_claim: Inline action menus surface AI verbs (shorten, expand, rephrase, summarize) at the cursor/selection. Lets users scope work at word/sentence/section/block granularity before invoking AI.
sources.relevance: Pattern reference for span-action menu in scene editor.

sources.title: Empty States — UserOnboard / Smashing Magazine / Toptal
sources.key_claim: Empty states are an onboarding opportunity, not chrome. Two parts instruction to one part delight. Best practice is to preload sample data or auto-generate starter content.
sources.relevance: Direct rationale for sample-world / template-prompt empty states across our 6 workspaces.

sources.title: React Router SPA & dynamic tab state preservation (reactrouter.com / GitHub discussions)
sources.key_claim: React Router supports nested layouts perfect for dashboard tabs. Tabs should preserve state when switching; Spotify mobile is the canonical 'route per tab, shared component' pattern.
sources.relevance: Architectural rationale for splitting our App.tsx monolith into a router-driven shell.

sources.title: NovelPad Review — Selfpublishing.com / Comfortable Shoes Studio
sources.key_claim: Cloud SPA. Chapters → scene cards with drag-and-drop reorder & auto-renumber; scene editor pops up over outline (half/full screen); ProWritingAid embedded; autosave every 60 seconds; realtime collab.
sources.relevance: Reference for 'jump-to-edit' from card view and autosave cadence.

sources.title: Unlock Your Writing Potential with NovelAI's Memory and Lorebooks — Eightify
sources.key_claim: NovelAI exposes the AI's working memory: current text window, active lorebook entries, author's note, memory injections. This transparency is something other tools lack.
sources.relevance: Pattern reference for our 'context inspector' pill — strongest AI-transparency pattern in survey.

sources.title: Dabble Plot Grid — Help Center / Reedsy / Kindlepreneur
sources.key_claim: Plot Grid is columns (Plot Lines) × rows (Scenes/Chapters) × cards (Plot Points), with drag-and-drop. Inspired by Harry Potter plotting method. Cleanest visual cross-cutting subplot tracker.
sources.relevance: Reference for branch/timeline workspace: cross-cutting subplot view.

sources.title: Aeon Timeline & Campfire — Mythic Scribes / Reedsy / Kindlepreneur
sources.key_claim: Aeon Timeline combines timeline + spreadsheet + mindmap + 'narrative view' (drag chronological events into non-chronological reading order). Campfire offers 17 modules sold individually (Characters, Maps, Magic, Languages...).
sources.relevance: Narrative-view pattern (chronological vs. reading order projection) directly applicable to our branch/timeline workspace.

sources.title: Make Canvas work more like a mind-map — Sudowrite Feedback
sources.key_claim: Users publicly request that Canvas behave more like a true mind-map, allowing connections between character cards and plot lines, with the ability to generate parallel and converging plotlines.
sources.relevance: Validates that unconstrained 2D canvases need stronger structural opinions to feel useful.

sources.title: Novelcrafter Codex Documentation
sources.key_claim: Codex auto-detects references in text; clicking shows a preview popover; prompts auto-pull referenced entries into context. Per-scene curation of which entries the model sees.
sources.relevance: Reference for auto-mention detection and per-scene context selection in our scene workspace.

sources.title: Scrivener Learning Curve Forums — Literature & Latte / Quora / Kindlepreneur
sources.key_claim: Scrivener's UI is described as a '747 cockpit', a 'patchwork of basic functionality with miscellaneous add-ons', no consistent UI between components. Compile/export is universally hated.
sources.relevance: Anti-pattern: visual-consistency drift across panels. Validates need for shared shell across our 6 workspaces.

sources.title: Best Novel Writing Software Compared (Chapter Blog / Legendfiction Substack)
sources.key_claim: Reviewer of 22 writing tools describes Novelcrafter as feeling 'a little dense, like there's just a bit too much going on visually', and Squibler as 'bulky and overbuilt for most writers'.
sources.relevance: Anti-pattern: visual density. Drives our 'density knob' recommendation in card layouts.

sources.title: World Anvil Review — Kindlepreneur / Self-Publishing School
sources.key_claim: World Anvil's strength is template-driven articles with embedded prompts ('describe the climate', 'describe daily life'). Weakness: extensive functionality is overwhelming, steep learning curve, hundreds of optional modules.
sources.relevance: Reference for embedded-prompt empty states in our world-building workspace; warning about module bloat.

sources.title: Worldwand — Premium Worldbuilding for Writers and GMs
sources.key_claim: Markets explicitly as 'a three-panel workspace built for creation and recall — browse structure on one side, edit in the center, and inspect detail, relationships, pending AI changes, or chat context on the other'.
sources.relevance: Confirms three-pane is the default expected layout for premium writer/GM tools in 2026.

sources.title: Designing Trustworthy AI Assistants: 9 Simple UX Patterns — Orange Loops
sources.key_claim: Trustworthy AI assistants need granular feedback (thumbs up/down per response, optional tags 'inaccurate', 'irrelevant'), display insights without overwhelming, and offer directional follow-ups (zoom in vs. zoom out).
sources.relevance: Pattern reference for AI critique/feedback surfacing without overwhelm.

sources.title: Three-Pane Layout in Editor / IDE Tools — Firefox DevTools 3-pane mode docs / Polypane Workspaces
sources.key_claim: The three-pane layout (browse | edit | inspect) is the dominant pattern across editor IDEs (VSCode, Firefox DevTools, Polypane) — preserves three persistent contexts: where you are, what you're working on, and what's true about it.
sources.relevance: Architectural rationale for our shell.

sources.title: AI UX Patterns | Regenerate — Shape of AI
sources.key_claim: Regenerate has two design modes: overwrite (lose previous) vs branching (each retry is a separate version). For creative work, branching with cyclable history is the strong pattern; overwrite-only is widely disliked.
sources.relevance: Direct reference for branch/timeline workspace: regenerate = branch, not overwrite.

sources.title: Autosave Patterns — UI-Patterns.com / Pajamas (GitLab) / Primer (GitHub)
sources.key_claim: Autosave UX: show 'Saving…' (with spinner) or 'Saved' + relative timestamp. For long-form, prefer 'Draft saved 1 min ago'. Don't remove the Save button — users panic without one.
sources.relevance: Direct guidance for our autosave & draft-vs-saved pattern.

sources.title: Plot Bunni GitHub README (MangoLion/plotbunni)
sources.key_claim: FOSS Novel Writing Suite with AI assistance, IndexedDB local storage, no accounts, no telemetry; works offline.
sources.relevance: Reference architecture for fully local-first AI writing app.

sources.title: Don't Prioritize Efficiency Over Expectations — Nielsen Norman Group
sources.key_claim: When implementing autosave, users still expect (and reach for) a Save action — meeting that expectation matters more than removing a redundant click.
sources.relevance: NN/G principle backing the 'keep the Save button' recommendation.

sources.title: Sudowrite Story Bible Even More Features changelog
sources.key_claim: Story Bible has been iterated heavily since launch — adding new fields, character templates, and worldbuilding sub-sections — showing it's the most-actively-evolved part of Sudowrite.
sources.relevance: Indicates that structured-context store is a long-term competitive moat in this category.

sources.title: Information Architecture Articles (NN/G topic page)
sources.key_claim: NN/G's IA research emphasizes mental models, navigation hierarchy, and 'cognitive walkthrough' as the core methods for designing complex tool IA.
sources.relevance: Methodological backing for our 5 IA principles synthesis.
